reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully formalizes the informal claim. B being a regular local ring is encoded via `[IsRegularLocalRing B]` (defined correctly as dim m/m² = dim R). I is declared as `(I : Ideal B)`. "B/I is Gorenstein" is encoded as `[IsGorensteinRing (B ⧸ I)]`; since B is local, B/I is local, and for local Noetherian rings the general `IsGorensteinRing` condition (localization at every maximal ideal is Gorenstein local) is equivalent to `IsGorensteinLocalRing` because B/I is isomorphic to its localization at the unique maximal ideal. "Not a local complete intersection" is correctly given by `¬ IsLocalCompleteIntersectionRing (B ⧸ I)` (Stacks 09Q3 formulation via completions and regular sequences). The conclusion `I.height ≠ 0 ∧ I.height ≠ 1` in `ℕ∞` precisely expresses "I cannot have height 0 or 1". Quantifier structure and types all match.
